All right, welcome back in this lesson. We are going to go over how to connect our pre-customers to their sense of power. So with safety well-established and reinforced, like we spoke about in the last lesson, connecting one to their sense of power becomes the next most important state, meta-state, that we can really connect people to. So the core premise of this behind everything we're gonna learn in this lesson is that empowerment isn't a zero-sum game. So you don't boost your power by taking it from them. Otherwise you end up with fearful, traumatized, powerless customers who won't all of a sudden magically regain it on the other side of the call once it's time to actually implement and get a result. So you'll just end up paying for it there, right? And customers who aren't empowered to implement aren't empowered and resourceful to take action. And these are the people who will eventually and rightfully turn on you, right? With chargebacks, with being what you would call a toxic customer in your membership area, the one who just kind of in your experience ruins the vibe for everyone else, but in fact they shouldn't have been there to begin with, right? They ended up in your program due to forced compliance, not an empowered yes. So we want to treat empowerment as something that is mutually reciprocated, mutually elevated through one another's presence, and it's certainly not something you gain more of by extracting it from someone else. That is really the definition of being predatory. So one of the greatest mistakes people make in sales is trying to make the other person feel lost, scared, and disempowered without them, right? And beyond being unethical, those states run totally counter to someone's confidence to make a sizable investment in your program. So decisions, especially good decisions, can't be made from a powerless state. So disempowerment is a learning disability and a handicap to positive action. Only compliance can be forced from this state of powerlessness. So rather, you want them to feel a combination of power, confidence, and control, which are all conducive to making daring, life-changing, bold, somewhat risky, daring decisions, right? So the goal is to have them get in touch with the most empowered version of themselves in your presence, right? Which is what creates resonance. So how do you amplify their power in your presence? How do you amplify the goodness and the empowerment they feel with you, right? And we all love being around those who we feel most powerful and empowered with, right? It only makes sense. So that is what we are seeking to establish here. And there are three core ways that we are going to do that. So one, honor or acknowledge where they've yet to give themselves credit. Next, ask questions that give them a chance to explore their own power. And then finally, give them as much choice and decision-making power as reasonably possible. So let's go a little bit deeper into all three of these. So number one, honor or acknowledge where they've yet to give themselves credit. So most people, including myself, are harsh self-critics that are so quick to condemn themselves and so slow to honor and celebrate themselves. So where it is genuine, where it is sincere, you want to give them a chance to reflect and say, yeah, I guess I was pretty fucking awesome in that moment. So what might that look like? What you're doing, trying to start a new freelancing career while raising two young kids, it's fucking incredible. Of course it's challenging. And I know it must be hard seeing people with not even 10% of what you have on your plate succeeding. But the amount of courage and commitment that you've already shown is the rare gift you have that will really serve you moving forward. And I want to honor you for that. Give someone a chance to see how powerful they already are, where they actually did something freaking awesome and have yet to give themselves their own pat on the back. Next, ask questions that give them a chance to explore and unpack their power. And in doing so, they can be reminded of where they're already powerful, where they're already successful and confident while tapping into the previously unclaimed and unexplored capabilities and capacities that led to it. So self discovery and discovering these in your presence leads once again to both empowerment, them feeling more empowered, and them feeling more resonance with you, right? Being the person who is present that helped them unlock this new level of self discovery in the most amazing way. So how do we do this? Simple, we ask, right? That's incredible. How do you think you did that? Give someone a chance to explore this incredible achievement that they maybe haven't even had a chance or opportunity to unpack themselves, right? What's been the biggest breakthrough you've had on this topic so far, right? If they're so caught in the struggle of not being where they're at, right? But they have in fact made significant strides on that journey. They're just not at that final destination yet. Give them a chance, right? To know and recognize and realize and celebrate the breakthroughs they've already had. What do you think has most contributed to the success you've already had in your executive career, right? What are the virtues that they're not celebrating? What are the virtues that they've yet to discover about themselves? Give them a chance and opportunity and gift them the opportunity to know what those are. Most people struggle to pull that off. What do you think was the difference maker for you? Once again, giving someone a chance to yield these new self-discoveries that empower them to move forward into deeper, greater opportunities. Next, give them as much choice and decision-making power as possible. So people will feel more safe and therefore powerfully engaged when they feel they've chosen the direction of the conversation and the parameters of the offer. And there is a way to do this, right? Without letting them dictate every element of it, right? Give them a sense of control, a sense of choice and a sense of decision-making power while still staying true to the agenda and the direction of the call that you're setting forth for them and what the offer is or isn't. So what this looks like, permission at critical junctures. So after presenting the agenda for the call, simply ask, how does that feel? Does it feel good to move forward exploring this? Give them a chance to dictate that pace, right? Are we gonna go forward in this direction or are we not? How do they feel in going forward rather than us just imposing and moving forward for them? Before you actually show them what the offer is, ask for permission. Would you like to get a preview of how we partner together inside this program? Once again, rather than imposing and giving them all this information that they've yet to consent to, right? Would it feel good to explore enrollment options at this time? Once again, asking for consent instead of diving into what may be viewed or deemed or experienced as a pressurized, scary, fearful moment for them. And then of course, where would you like to go from here? So if you've presented options to them on how to work together, you could ask them, where would you like to go? And this is once again permission for them to guide to where that conversation goes. So asking for permission at critical junctures is a massive way to create both safety and a sense of being empowered on the sales call rather than just kind of being pulled by the neck to wherever you feel you need to lead them, right? It's not about you needing to lead them. It's about you adapting to where they're at, to getting buy-in and permission and consent, especially before the critical junctures, where there's going to be a little bit more charge or potential more charge around how that part of the conversation is received. Next, we've got the co-creative close, right? So have them create as much of the terms as they can, right? So you can ask, if you decide to move forward today, I want this to feel as awesome as possible, right? What would make that possible for you? So this is where you invite them to co-create some of those elements, right? And you give them a little bit of leniency, right? Within reason and within what is in integrity for you and your company, but give them a chance to state what would make it feel great, whether that's different payment plans, bonuses, a starting date to the program, have them invest themselves in what that offer might actually look like, right? So these are all options we have on the table for creating safety, for establishing it, for generating it, and for reinforcing it throughout the major and most important junctures of your sales call. So put these into practice, and when you're ready, Mimi, in the next lesson, we are where we are gonna talk about connecting our prospects to their sense of power and their expanded vision. So take a breather and I'll catch you there.
